key_components: AsyncAnthropic client management with lazy loading
Portuguese-language system prompt for Catholic theological expertise
Response parsing with regex-based JSON extraction
Integration with ValidationResult and Citation data models

patterns_discovered.description: API client (_client) created on first use via _get_client() method
patterns_discovered.benefit: Defers instantiation until needed, reduces initialization overhead

patterns_discovered.description: All I/O operations use async/await with AsyncAnthropic
patterns_discovered.benefit: Non-blocking API calls suitable for async web framework

patterns_discovered.description: Settings and API key pulled from get_settings() with parameter override support
JSON extraction via regex with complete default ValidationResult on failure
patterns_discovered.benefit: Graceful degradation rather than exception propagation

patterns_discovered.description: Full use of Python 3.10+ type annotations with Union types
Comprehensive system prompt in Portuguese defining JSON schema and theological context
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear instructions reduce hallucinations and ensure consistent output format

gotchas_discovered.description: API key validation only occurs on first validate() call, not during initialization. Missing key will crash at runtime.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Developer won't know about configuration issues until validation attempt

gotchas_discovered.description: Regex pattern `\{[\s\S]*\}` assumes valid JSON is always present in response. Markdown formatting variations could cause extraction failure.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Falls back to default result (score=0.5) on any regex mismatch, losing validation attempt

gotchas_discovered.description: Code assumes Claude returns score in 0-100 range, divides by 100 to normalize to 0-1. No validation of actual range returned.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Score could be incorrect if API returns unexpected value

gotchas_discovered.description: Model 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514' is hardcoded with no easy override mechanism for production model updates
gotchas_discovered.impact: May become outdated; requires code change to upgrade model

gotchas_discovered.description: API calls have no timeout specified, could hang indefinitely on network issues
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could cause resource exhaustion under network problems

gotchas_discovered.description: validate() method re-raises all exceptions from API calls, no fallback graceful handling
gotchas_discovered.impact: API failures will crash the caller; no resilience for temporary outages

gotchas_discovered.description: No built-in rate limiting or request throttling for API calls
gotchas_discovered.impact: Could trigger rate limit errors or unexpected costs from burst requests

gotchas_discovered.description: JSON parsing failure uses logger.warning instead of logger.error despite returning degraded result
gotchas_discovered.impact: May not alert operators to validation failures

approach_outcome.summary: FallbackValidationProvider successfully implements AI-based Catholic theological validation using Claude API as a fallback when NotebookLM is unavailable
requirements_met: Uses Claude/GPT for AI-based theological validation
Includes system prompt with Catholic theological context (Catecismo, Magistério references)
Generates flag list for doctrinal issues
Always returns provider='ai_fallback' to indicate non-authoritative source
Integrates with ValidationResult and Citation models
approach_outcome.code_quality: High - well-structured, type-hinted, follows async patterns
approach_outcome.integration: Clean integration with existing validation service infrastructure
approach_outcome.completeness: Fully functional implementation ready for use

recommendations.recommendation: Add timeout parameter to API calls (e.g., 30-60 seconds)
recommendations.rationale: Prevent indefinite hangs on network issues

recommendations.recommendation: Implement graceful degradation for API failures instead of re-raising exceptions
recommendations.rationale: Validation should return degraded but usable result rather than crash the application

recommendations.recommendation: Implement retry logic with exponential backoff for transient failures
Replace regex JSON extraction with structured output if API supports it
recommendations.rationale: More reliable than regex parsing; reduces edge cases

recommendations.recommendation: Make model name and max_tokens configurable via settings
recommendations.rationale: Allow environment-specific customization without code changes

recommendations.recommendation: Add logging for successful validations and API response metrics
recommendations.rationale: Enable monitoring of fallback provider usage and performance

recommendations.recommendation: Add input validation for content length and content_type parameters
recommendations.rationale: Prevent unexpectedly large requests or invalid content types

recommendations.recommendation: Add unit tests for _parse_response() with edge cases (malformed JSON, missing fields, out-of-range scores)
recommendations.rationale: Ensure robust JSON parsing under various failure scenarios

recommendations.recommendation: Document why Portuguese system prompt was chosen and when it should be localized
recommendations.rationale: Clarify design decision for future maintainers

recommendations.recommendation: Track fallback provider usage to understand NotebookLM availability patterns
recommendations.rationale: Identify systemic issues with primary provider
